question:
Will Argentina win Group J in the 2026 FIFA World Cup?

Resolution criteria: Resolves to the outcome of the question found at https://polymarket.com/market/will-argentina-win-group-j-in-the-2026-fifa-world-cup.

Background: This market will resolve according to the team that wins Group J in the 2026 FIFA World Cup group stage, scheduled for June 11-27, 2026.

If multiple teams tie as group winners, this market will resolve according to the official tiebreak procedure of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If the World Cup group stage is cancelled, postponed after September 30, 2026, or there is otherwise no winner declared for this group within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from FIFA (https://www.fifa.com/); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

evidences.title: World Cup Betting: Latest on FIFA WC 2026 - Covers.com
evidences.content: Group E — Germany (-200): The Germans are proven winners and should be able to navigate a group featuring Ecuador and the Ivory Coast. Group F — Netherlands (-139): The class of Group F. Netherlands' offense should get them out of any sticky situation. Group G — Belgium (-227): Belgium's Golden Generation may be over, but they are the class of Group G. Group H — Spain (-333): Spain are the favorite to win it all for a reason. Don't expect a Group stage slip-up. Group I — France (-143): France have the experience which gives them the edge over a very impressive Norway side. Group J — Argentina (-227): The defending Champions should make quick work of Austria and Algeria to top Group J. [...] Argentina (+800): The defending champions are well-equipped to defend their title even if Lionel Messi doesn't feature prominently. They've got an elite defense and a solid goalkeeper, which goes a long way in tournament football. ### World Cup Group Winners Odds & Favorites Group A — Mexico (+120): Mexico came away with a great group draw and should have no problems navigating the Korea Republic and South Africa. evidences.title: Qualified teams for the FIFA World Cup 2026
evidences.content: ### ArgentinaArgentina The reigning world champions were the first CONMEBOL team to qualify. They secured their spot on 25 March when when Bolivia drew 0-0 with Uruguay. That result meant Argentina were guaranteed to finish in one of South America's six automatic World Cup qualification spots. Image 6: Lionel Messi of Argentina celebrates with his teammates after scoring the fifth goal of his team during the FIFA World Cup 2026 Qualifier match between Argentina and Bolivia at Estadio Mas Monumental Antonio Vespucio in Buenos Aires, Argentina, on October 15, 2024. (Photo by Daiana Panza/NurPhoto via Getty Images) ### UzbekistanUzbekistan Uzbekistan reached their first World Cup after securing a top-two finish in the third round of Asian qualifying on 5 June. Defending World Cup champions Argentina are your favorites and around minus 265. And I mean, it's Argentina. It's Messi. They cruised in qualifying. Messi was the leading goal scorer in CONMEBOL qualifying. It is what it is. But, Austria is next at plus 370 to win group J. This is kind of a sneaky good team. They won their qualifying group six wins, one draw, one defeat. Good players here. Sabitzer, Konrad Laimer, David Alaba still as well. So, very interesting team there. Third, Algeria plus 800. evidences.title: 2026 FIFA World Cup Odds — Who Will Win? - Polycopy
evidences.content: NOV 2025 ### Group Draw — Markets Reprice The official group draw assigns all 48 teams to 12 groups. Draw results immediately reprice the winner market — a favorable group path can bump a contender's odds 1-2%, while a "group of death" compresses their price. Historically the biggest single-day repricing event before the tournament begins. JUN 11, 2026 ### Group Stage Begins — 104 Matches Across 3 Nations The group stage runs approximately two weeks with matches across the USA, Mexico, and Canada. Each group plays 6 matches, and results cascade through the winner market. Early upsets create the tournament's most volatile trading windows — favorites stumbling in their opener see dramatic price drops.
